findings.title: A `update_session_status(...).await.ok()` path was missed in the same handler
findings.description: This diff replaces silent error swallowing for `update_session_status`/`update_last_active` in `process_turn_events`, but `message.rs` still has another `update_session_status(...).await.ok()` in the session-eviction path. That means DB write failures during eviction remain invisible, even though they can leave the evicted thread marked as active/running and desynchronize in-memory session maps from persisted state.
findings.suggestion: Apply the same `if let Err(e) = ... { tracing::warn!(...) }` pattern here so all session-state writes in this handler are observable and handled consistently.

findings.title: TTL cleanup still swallows session status update failures
findings.description: The TTL sweep removes in-memory state and then calls `update_session_status(...).await.ok()` for each evicted thread. If SQLite is unavailable, the bot will notify Discord that a session was cleaned up while the database may still say otherwise. For a Discord bot, that kind of state skew is operationally significant because later routing or recovery logic may treat the thread as still active.
findings.suggestion: Replace this `.ok()` with the same warn-logging pattern used in the diff, or funnel all best-effort session-status writes through a helper that logs failures uniformly.

findings.title: Background session manager still has multiple silent DB state write failures
findings.description: There are several remaining `update_session_status(...).await.ok()` calls in the background worker loop (`running` and multiple `idle` transitions). These are arguably the highest-risk places to keep swallowing errors: if a write fails while a background turn exits or aborts, the persisted session status can remain stuck in `running`, which is exactly the kind of stale state that blocks future turns or misrepresents bot activity. The current patch improves one completion path but leaves the same failure mode elsewhere in the bot's state machine.
findings.suggestion: Convert the remaining background-loop status writes to explicit logging as well. A small helper for 'best-effort session status update with warn logging' would reduce repetition and keep behavior consistent across normal, TTL, eviction, and background-turn paths.

findings.title: The new logging pattern is repetitive enough to merit a helper
findings.description: The diff repeats the same `if let Err(e) = repository::update_session_status/update_last_active ... { tracing::warn!(...) }` block many times. That is correct locally, but this codebase already has other missed `.ok()` call sites, which suggests repetition is making consistency harder to maintain. In a bot with several turn-exit branches, a dedicated helper would make the intended 'best-effort but observable' policy explicit.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a small helper such as `warn_on_db_error(op, thread_id, future)` or dedicated wrappers around `update_session_status` and `update_last_active`, then use that helper everywhere these writes are intentionally non-fatal.

summary: The change is directionally correct, but it is incomplete: several `update_session_status(...).await.ok()` call sites outside this diff still silently drop DB failures and can leave Discord-facing bot state inconsistent with SQLite.
